title: festuca aspera
intro: Festuca aspera, also known as the rough fescue, is a type of grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its coarse texture and distinctive growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Festuca aspera is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or decoction, and can be combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and salves for topical application.
